# pyIsEmail

{{ pypi }} {{ ci }} {{ coveralls }} {{ downloads }}

## Getting Started

pyIsEmail is a no-nonsense approach for checking whether that
user-supplied email address could be real. Sick of not being able to use
[email address tagging] to sort through your [Bacn]? We can fix that.

Regular expressions are cheap to write, but often require maintenance when
new top-level domains come out or don't conform to email addressing
features that come back into vogue. pyIsEmail allows you to validate an
email address -- and even check the domain, if you wish -- with one simple
call, making your code more readable and faster to write. When you want to
know why an email address doesn't validate, we even provide you with
a diagnosis.

## Install

Install from PyPI using [pip], a package manager for Python.

```bash
$ pip install pyIsEmail
```

Don't have pip installed? Try installing it by running this from the
command line:

```bash
$ curl https://raw.github.com/pypa/pip/master/contrib/get-pip.py | python
```

Or you can [download the source code (zip)] for `pyIsEmail` and then
run:

```bash
$ python setup.py install
```

You may need to run the above commands with `sudo`.

## Usage

For the simplest usage, import and use the `is_email` function:

```python
from pyisemail import is_email

address = "test@example.com"
bool_result = is_email(address)
detailed_result = is_email(address, diagnose=True)
```

You can also check whether the domain used in the email is a valid domain
and whether or not it has a valid MX record:

```python
from pyisemail import is_email

address = "test@example.com"
bool_result_with_dns = is_email(address, check_dns=True)
detailed_result_with_dns = is_email(address, check_dns=True, diagnose=True)
```

These are primary indicators of whether an email address can even be
issued at that domain. However, a valid response here *is not a guarantee
that the email exists*, merely that is *can* exist.

If you want to limit using a [gTLD] as the domain part of the email
address, you can do so with a flag:

```python
from pyisemail import is_email

address = "thiswont@workatall"
bool_result_with_check = is_email(address, allow_gtld=False)
detailed_result_with_check = is_email(address, allow_gtld=False, diagnose=True)
```

In addition to the base `is_email` functionality, you can also use the
validators by themselves. Check the validator source doe to see how this
works.

## Uninstall

Want to get rid of pyIsEmail? Did you install with pip? Here you go:

```bash
$ pip uninstall pyIsEmail
```

## Acknowledgements

The base `ParserValidator` is based off of [Dominic Sayers]' [is_email
script][is_email script]. I wanted the functionality in Python, so I ported it from the
original PHP.

## Contributing

So you're interested in contributing to pyIsEmail? Check out our [contributing guidelines](./CONTRIBUTING.rst) for more information on how to do that.

## Versioning

This library aims to adhere to [Semantic Versioning 2.0.0]. Violations of
this scheme should be reported as bugs.

functions.name: pyisemail.utils.enum
documentation:
Provide the capabilities of an enum from other languages.

Keyword arguments:
enums --- name/value pairs of arguments for enum names/values

functions.name: pyisemail.is_email
documentation:
Validate an email address.

Keyword arguments:
address   --- the email address as a string
check_dns --- flag for whether to check the DNS status of the domain
diagnose  --- flag for whether to return True/False or a Diagnosis
allow_gtld --- flag for whether to prevent gTLDs as the domain

classes.name: pyisemail.reference.Reference
documentation:
A container for diagnosis references, for more information.

Holds the citation in the pertinent RFC, as well as a link to the specific
section of the RFC being referred to.

methods.name: pyisemail.validators.dns_validator.DNSValidator.is_valid
documentation:
Check whether a domain has a valid MX or A record.

Keyword arguments:
domain   --- the domain to check
diagnose --- flag to report a diagnosis or a boolean (default False)

methods.name: pyisemail.validators.gtld_validator.GTLDValidator.is_valid
documentation:
Check whether a domain is a gTLD.

Keyword arguments:
domain   --- the domain to check
diagnose --- flag to report a diagnosis or a boolean (default False)

classes.name: pyisemail.email_validator.EmailValidator

methods.name: pyisemail.email_validator.EmailValidator.is_email
documentation:
Interface for is_email method.

Keyword arguments:
address    -- address to check.
diagnose   -- flag to report a diagnose or just True/False

Abstract email validator to subclass from.

You should not instantiate an EmailValidator, as it merely provides the
interface for is_email, not an implementation.

classes.name: pyisemail.diagnosis.deprecated_diagnosis.DeprecatedDiagnosis
documentation: A diagnosis indicating the presence of deprecated address features.

classes.name: pyisemail.diagnosis.rfc5321_diagnosis.RFC5321Diagnosis
documentation: A diagnosis indicating the address is only valid for SMTP.

classes.name: pyisemail.diagnosis.dns_diagnosis.DNSDiagnosis
documentation: A diagnosis indicating a lack of a DNS record for a domain.

classes.name: pyisemail.diagnosis.cfws_diagnosis.CFWSDiagnosis
documentation: A diagnosis indicating a problem with white space in the address.

classes.name: pyisemail.diagnosis.invalid_diagnosis.InvalidDiagnosis
documentation: A diagnosis indicating the presence of an invalid address component.

classes.name: pyisemail.diagnosis.rfc5322_diagnosis.RFC5322Diagnosis
documentation: A diagnosis indicating the address is only technically valid.

classes.name: pyisemail.diagnosis.gtld_diagnosis.GTLDDiagnosis
documentation: A diagnosis indicating that a domain is a disallowed gTLD.

classes.name: pyisemail.diagnosis.base_diagnosis.BaseDiagnosis
documentation:
Super class for an error diagnosis.

You should rarely, i.e. only in testing, instantiate a BaseDiagnosis, as
it does not provide any pertinent information. Always use one of its
subclasses.

classes.name: pyisemail.diagnosis.valid_diagnosis.ValidDiagnosis
documentation: A diagnosis indicating the address is valid for use.

functions.name: pyisemail.validators.parser_validator.to_char
documentation:
Transforms the ASCII control character symbols to their real char.

Note: If the token is not an ASCII control character symbol, just
return the token.

Keyword arguments:
token -- the token to transform

methods.name: pyisemail.validators.parser_validator.ParserValidator.is_email
documentation:
Check that an address address conforms to RFCs 5321, 5322 and others.

More specifically, see the follow RFCs:
    * http://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc5321
    * http://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc5322
    * http://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc4291#section-2.2
    * http://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc1123#section-2.1
    * http://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3696) (guidance only)

Keyword arguments:
address    -- address to check.
diagnose   -- flag to report a diagnosis or a boolean (default False)
